public String reverseVowels(String s) {
    Set<Character> vowelsSet = new HashSet<>(Arrays.asList('a', 'e', 'i', 'o', 'u'));
    List<Character> vowelsInString = new ArrayList<>();
    for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
        char c = s.charAt(i);
        if (vowelsSet.contains(Character.toLowerCase(c))) {
            vowelsInString.add(c);
        }
    }
    Collections.reverse(vowelsInString);
    StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
    int ptr = 0;
    for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
        char c = s.charAt(i);
        if (vowelsSet.contains(Character.toLowerCase(c))) {
            sb.append(vowelsInString.get(ptr++));
        } else {
            sb.append(c);
        }
    }
    return sb.toString();
}

Efficiency is HIGH since it's two passes and O(n) time.

public String reverseVowels(String s) {
    Set<Character> vowelsSet = new HashSet<>(Arrays.asList('a', 'e', 'i', 'o', 'u'));
    List<Character> vowelsInString = new ArrayList<>();
    for (char c : s.toCharArray()) {

        if (vowelsSet.contains(Character.toLowerCase(c))) {
            sb.append(vowelsInString.get(ptr++));
        } else {
            sb.append(c);
        }
    }
    return sb.toString();
}

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public String reverseVowels(String s) {
        Set<Character> vowelsSet = new HashSet<>(Arrays.asList('a', 'e', 'i', 'o', 'u'));
        List<Character> vowelsInString = new ArrayList<>();
        for (char c : s.toCharArray()) {
            if (vowelsSet.contains(Character.toLowerCase(c))) {
                vowelsInString.add(c);
            }
        }
        Collections.reverse(vowelsInString);
        StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
        int ptr = 0;
        for (char c : s.toCharArray()) {
            if (vowelsSet.contains(Character.toLowerCase(c))) {
                sb.append(vowelsInString.get(ptr++));
            } else {
                sb.append(c);
            }
        }
        return sb.toString();
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution collects all vowels in the string in O(n) time, reverses the list, and reconstructs the string by replacing vowels in reverse order. It uses two passes over the string and O(n) auxiliary space for the vowel list and result. The approach is efficient and handles case sensitivity correctly.
